parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: 7 Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0
heading_text: 7 Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Minor updates to the introduction were made to broaden the use cases and describe the relationship to older versions of the SDTM.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: A concept map showing the organization of the model was also added.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: In examples of qualifier types, specific variable names have been replaced with descriptions because the variables are introduced later in the document.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The description of Synonym Qualifiers was updated to reflect that some variables are synonym qualifiers of multiple variables.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The relationships domain Related Specimens (RELSPEC) was added.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Sections were reordered and regrouped, which resulted in the renumbering of sections and the elimination of some unnecessary section layers.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The metadata tables now have Variable(s) Qualified, Usage Restrictions, Variable C-code, Definition, and Examples columns.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The Notes column replaces the former Description column.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: A section called Table Structure has been added to show the variable metadata included in the tables.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The section header Version History was removed.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: It was an unnecessary extra layer, because this section deals only with the changes from one version to the next.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The Variable, Dataset, and Section Changes and Additions subsection was removed, as this was also an unnecessary extra layer.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Table numbers were removed, as they are redundant with section numbers.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Usage restrictions of "Not in nonclinical trials" were added.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: A new section, Study Subject Data, was created which groups the general observation classes and special-purpose domains.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The variable APID was removed from Section 3.1.4, Identifiers for All Classes, because APID is only allowed in AP domains.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: It is still listed in new Section 4, Associated Persons Data.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The variables qualified for --DOSFRM, --DOSFRQ, and --DOSRGM were changed from --DOSE, --DOSTXT, and --DOSTOT to --TRT.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Because the role of the domain-specific variable --BEATNO was changed to Identifier, it was moved from Section 3.1.3, The Findings Observation Class, to Section 3.1.4, Identifiers for All Classes.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: --LNKID and --LNKGRP have been revised to clarify that the relationships are within a subject.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The special-purpose Subject Visits (SV) domain remains a special-purpose domain, but additional variables have been added to address the effect of an epidemic or pandemic on visits.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The role of the --LOINC variable was changed from Synonym Qualifier of --TESTCD to Record Qualifier.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: A single variable of --TESTCD may be associated with multiple values of --LOINC, depending on the values of other variables, such as --SPEC and --METHOD.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The order of --METHOD was changed so that the variable appears after --PORTOT, rather than after --LOC.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The Trial Design Model has been reorganized and revised.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Text in the top-level section was revised to replace a specific list of trial design concepts with a more general statement about the use of variables from trial design domains being used in subject-level domains.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: The role of the Trial Disease Milestones variable TMDEF was changed to Variable Qualifier with MIDSTYPE as the variable qualified.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: New variables have been added to the following sections.

parent_section: §7 [Changes from SDTM v1.8 to SDTM v2.0]
verbatim: Many of these are restricted to use in particular domains, as noted in the Usage Restrictions column.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: 8 Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM
heading_text: 8 Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The following changes are proposed for the next version of the SDTM:

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The variable --BLFL will be considered for deprecation as a qualifier for findings class domains.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The variable --MODIFY will be considered for deprecation as a qualifier for findings class domains. The variable --MODIFY will remain as a qualifier for the events and interventions classes.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The findings variable --BODSYS may be restricted to use in nonclinical trials.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The variable TIRL will be considered for removal from the Trial Inclusion/Exclusion (TI) domain.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The variable --TSTDTL may be made domain-specific, for use only in the Microbiology Specimen (MB), Microscopic Findings (MI), and Genetic Findings (GF) domains.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The findings variable --LOBXFL was introduced in SDTM v1.7.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: In the accompanying SDTMIG v3.3, for domains where --BLFL had been included as an expected variable, --BLFL was made permissible, while --LOBXFL was added as an expected variable.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: Future deprecation was not announced in SDTM v1.7 in order to provide a sufficient transition period.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: Deprecation in the next version of the SDTM with an accompanying SDTMIG is proposed as this should provide a sufficient transition period.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: As a findings variable, --MODIFY was originally included for use in the Physical Examination (PE) domain in the SDTMIG to support coding of an abnormality recorded as a result of a physical examination.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: However, abnormalities found during a physical examination are now more commonly recorded as events in the Medical History (MH) domain or the Adverse Events (AE) domain, where the full range of coding variables are available.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: This is consistent with the best-practice scenario described in the Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization Implementation Guide (CDASHIG).

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: After --MODIFY is deprecated as a findings qualifier, an implementer coding abnormalities found at a physical exam will not be able to represent the coding in PE domain, but will be able to represent coding in the appropriate events domain.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: No use cases other than in the PE domain have been identified for --MODIFY as a findings variable.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The rationale for removing --BODSYS as a qualifier for use in clinical trials is the same as the rationale for removing --MODIFY.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: If deprecated, the variable will still be available for use in nonclinical trials, where it has been used to classify the body system of the organ represented as a value of --SPEC.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: The variable TIRL was included in the Trial Inclusion/Exclusion Criteria (TI) domain in anticipation of developing a way to represent eligibility criteria in a computer-executable manner.

parent_section: §8 [Proposed Future Changes to the SDTM]
verbatim: However, such a method has not been developed, and it is not clear that an SDTM dataset would be the best place to represent such a computer-executable representation.

parent_section: §9 [Appendices]
verbatim: 9 Appendices

parent_section: §9 [Appendices]
verbatim: Appendix A: Representations and Warranties, Limitations of Liability, and Disclaimers
heading_text: Appendix A: Representations and Warranties, Limitations of Liability, and Disclaimers

parent_section: Appendix A: Representations and Warranties, Limitations of Liability, and Disclaimers
verbatim: CDISC Patent Disclaimers

parent_section: CDISC Patent Disclaimers
verbatim: It is possible that implementation of and compliance with this standard may require use of subject matter covered by patent rights.

parent_section: CDISC Patent Disclaimers
verbatim: By publication of this standard, no position is taken with respect to the existence or validity of any claim or of any patent rights in connection therewith.

parent_section: CDISC Patent Disclaimers
verbatim: CDISC, including the CDISC Board of Directors, shall not be responsible for identifying patent claims for which a license may be required in order to implement this standard or for conducting inquiries into the legal validity or scope of those patents or patent claims that are brought to its attention.

parent_section: Appendix A: Representations and Warranties, Limitations of Liability, and Disclaimers
verbatim: Representations and Warranties

parent_section: Representations and Warranties
verbatim: "CDISC grants open public use of this User Guide (or Final Standards) under CDISC's copyright."

parent_section: Representations and Warranties
verbatim: Each Participant in the development of this standard shall be deemed to represent, warrant, and covenant, at the time of a Contribution by such Participant (or by its Representative), that to the best of its knowledge and ability: (a) it holds or has the right to grant all relevant licenses to any of its Contributions in all jurisdictions or territories in which it holds relevant intellectual property rights; (b) there are no limits to the Participant's ability to make the grants, acknowledgments, and agreements herein; and (c) the Contribution does not subject any Contribution, Draft Standard, Final Standard, or implementations thereof, in whole or in part, to licensing obligations with additional restrictions or requirements inconsistent with those set forth in this Policy, or that would require any such Contribution, Final Standard, or implementation, in whole or in part, to be either: (i) disclosed or distributed in source code form; (ii) licensed for the purpose of making derivative works (other than as set forth in Section 4.2 of the CDISC Intellectual Property Policy ("the Policy")); or (iii) distributed at no charge, except as set forth in Sections 3, 5.1, and 4.2 of the Policy.

parent_section: Representations and Warranties
verbatim: If a Participant has knowledge that a Contribution made by any Participant or any other party may subject any Contribution, Draft Standard, Final Standard, or implementation, in whole or in part, to one or more of the licensing obligations listed in Section 9.3, such Participant shall give prompt notice of the same to the CDISC President who shall promptly notify all Participants.

parent_section: Representations and Warranties
verbatim: No Other Warranties/Disclaimers. ALL PARTICIPANTS ACKNOWLEDGE THAT, EXCEPT AS PROVIDED UNDER SECTION 9.3 OF THE CDISC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY POLICY, ALL DRAFT STANDARDS AND FINAL STANDARDS, AND ALL CONTRIBUTIONS TO FINAL STANDARDS AND DRAFT STANDARDS, ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" WITH NO WARRANTIES WHATSOEVER, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, OR OTHERWISE, AND THE PARTICIPANTS, REPRESENTATIVES, THE CDISC PRESIDENT, THE CDISC BOARD OF DIRECTORS, AND CDISC EXPRESSLY DISCLAIM ANY WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY, NONINFRINGEMENT, FITNESS FOR ANY PARTICULAR OR INTENDED PURPOSE, OR ANY OTHER WARRANTY OTHERWISE ARISING OUT OF ANY PROPOSAL, FINAL STANDARDS OR DRAFT STANDARDS, OR CONTRIBUTION.

parent_section: Appendix A: Representations and Warranties, Limitations of Liability, and Disclaimers
verbatim: Limitation of Liability

parent_section: Limitation of Liability
verbatim: IN NO EVENT WILL CDISC OR ANY OF ITS CONSTITUENT PARTS (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE CDISC BOARD OF DIRECTORS, THE CDISC PRESIDENT, CDISC STAFF, AND CDISC MEMBERS) BE LIABLE TO ANY OTHER PERSON OR ENTITY FOR ANY LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF USE, DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, WHETHER UNDER CONTRACT, TORT, WARRANTY, OR OTHERWISE, ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THIS POLICY OR ANY RELATED AGREEMENT, WHETHER OR NOT SUCH PARTY HAD ADVANCE NOTICE OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.
